title: teams — retention windows
description: Active teams are permanent while any tenant references them. Team members are permanent as historic roster (compliance material — participation lineage). Trials are retained 3 years post-decision (anti-discrimination defence records for rejected trials; audit trail for converted trials). Event teams retained 7 years when payment_status is set (financial), 3 years otherwise (participation record). Anonymous trialist PII redacted 90 days post-decision.
compliance_regimes_referenced: gdpr Art. 5(1)(c) — data minimisation (anonymous trialist redaction)
gdpr Art. 17 — right to erasure (with financial-retention + anti-discrimination-retention carve-outs)
anti-discrimination — 3-year hold on rejected trials for defence records
national tax law — 7-year financial-record retention when trial fees paid

purge.trigger: archived + 730 days elapsed + zero downstream references
purge.notes: The observer refuses hard-delete while any team_members / team_trials / event_teams row exists (RESTRICT FK). Retention window aligns with the longest-lived downstream row (trials at 3 years for rejected + 7 years for financial).

cold.days: unbounded — historic roster is compliance material
cold.description: Terminal-state rows (inactive / graduated / suspended / injured) retained indefinitely as participation lineage. This is deliberate — reversing a hard-delete of a former-member row is impossible + destroys the historic roster.

purge.trigger: personal-erasure request (user delete themselves) → REDACT in place (name/phone/email cleared to [REDACTED]) but preserve the structural fact
purge.notes: GDPR Art. 17 vs anti-discrimination + compliance carve-out: the structural fact (team X had a member during period Y) is retained; the personal identifiers are redacted. Same pattern as User module.
Converted trials survive as TeamMember rows (which never expire). The trial row itself can be purged after 3 years — the audit trail continues to reference it.
Rejected trials are the primary defence record against discrimination claims. Retention is deliberately long. decision_notes is the material fact — clear, contemporaneous rationale.
No decision was actually made — retention window shorter than rejected.

purge.trigger: decided_at + 90 days for terminal-state trials (rejected / no_show / cancelled)
purge.notes: Preserves the trial row's structural fact (a trial happened, decision was X) + the price/payment fields for financial retention. Only the anonymous PII sub-fields are redacted. For CONVERTED trials the profile migrates into the resulting Athlete/User row + is cleared on the trial.
When event participation involves a trial fee OR (Wave 4) an event registration fee, financial retention applies.

cross_reference: The audit rows written by Auditable outlive the source rows per audit::retention.json — audit retention is 7 years regardless of source-row retention.

TenantErased cascades hard-delete of every teams / team_members / team_trials / event_teams row (FK cascade), but the audit rows written during those events survive the cascade (per audit module's outlives-source design).
